content.problemStatement:
Given a string SSS, find the longest substring that is a palindrome. A palindrome is a string that reads the same forwards and backwards. If multiple palindromic substrings have the same length, return the one that appears first in the string.

For example, if S="aaaabbaa"S = \text{"aaaabbaa"}S="aaaabbaa", the longest palindromic substring is "aabbaa"\text{"aabbaa"}"aabbaa".


Input Format

First line contains NNN - length of the string
Second line contains the string SSS


Output Format

Print the longest palindromic substring
If multiple solutions exist, print the leftmost one

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
String = "aaaabbaa"
"aabbaa" is the longest palindromic substring
Length = 6

Explanation:
String = "abc"
All single characters are palindromes
"a" is the leftmost palindrome of length 1

Explanation:
String = "abacdfgdcaba"
"aba" appears twice and is the longest palindrome
Return first occurrence from left
